title: fagraea picrophloea
intro: Fagraea picrophloea, also known as the white frangipani or white gardenia, is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Fagraea picrophloea include reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as ointments and creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
